title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Abuse, and Sensitive Business Flows · Execution Trace: Webhook Raw Signature
summary: A webhook parses JSON before verifying raw bytes, accepts replay forever, fetches callback URLs from users, retries non-idempotent work, and rate-limits requests but not expensive business actions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo webhook raw signature increment in webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.
Set content-type protection, framing, referrer, transport, content security, permissions, and cache policy according to whether responses are API data or HTML.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
